issue_body:
The "About" dialogue in GNOME currently shows the amount of memory actually visible by the OS, along with hardware information, such as the CPU and Graphics used. On a lot of systems with integrated video cards, the OS visible RAM doesn't match "hardware" RAM.

On x86, the DMI information contains useful data:
```
Handle 0x003C, DMI type 17, 40 bytes
Memory Device
        Array Handle: 0x003B
        Error Information Handle: Not Provided
        Total Width: 64 bits
        Data Width: 64 bits
        Size: 8 GB
        Form Factor: DIMM
        Set: None
        Locator: ChannelA-DIMM0
        Bank Locator: BANK 0
        Type: DDR4
        Type Detail: Synchronous
        Speed: 2667 MT/s
        Manufacturer: Samsung
        Serial Number: 416433E9
        Asset Tag: 9876543210
        Part Number: M378A1K43CB2-CTD    
        Rank: 1
        Configured Memory Speed: 2400 MT/s
        Minimum Voltage: 1.2 V
        Maximum Voltage: 1.2 V
        Configured Voltage: 1.2 V
<etc>
```

For each slot, having the amount, the form-factor, the type (DDRX) and the speed of the RAM could allow showing something like `24GiB 2600Mt/s DDR4 DIMM`, instead of `23.4GiB`, or could show the separate memory modules graphically, along with the free space if any (look for `about this mac` images, you can see the textual representation used in newer versions, and the graphical info used in older versions).

That information is available in `/sys/firmware/dmi/entries/17-*` on x86 Linux systems, but we don't have good "devices" in udev to attach this information to without encoding it somehow. I also don't know whether `hostnamectl` would be a good place to export this information (probably not).
pr_title: udev: Extract RAM properties from DMI information
pr_body:
Add memory_id program to set properties about the physical memory
devices in the system. This is useful on machines with removable memory
modules to show how the machine can be upgraded, and on all devices to
detect the actual RAM size, without relying on the OS accessible amount.

Closes: #16651

@felipeborges, I'd appreciate a test! :)

This is the output of the helper (so the properties that should be set on the DMI device):
```
MEMORY_ARRAY_LOCATION=System Board Or Motherboard
MEMORY_ARRAY_MAX_CAPACITY=68719476736
MEMORY_ARRAY_NUM_DEVICES=4
MEMORY_DEVICE_TOTAL_WIDTH0=64
MEMORY_DEVICE_DATA_WIDTH0=64
MEMORY_DEVICE_SIZE0=8589934592
MEMORY_DEVICE_FORM_FACTOR0=DIMM
MEMORY_DEVICE_LOCATOR0=ChannelA-DIMM0
MEMORY_DEVICE_BANK_LOCATOR0=BANK 0
MEMORY_DEVICE_TYPE0=DDR4
MEMORY_DEVICE_TYPE_DETAIL0=Synchronous
MEMORY_DEVICE_SPEED_MTS0=2667
MEMORY_DEVICE_MANUFACTURER0=Samsung
MEMORY_DEVICE_SERIAL_NUMBER0=416433E9
MEMORY_DEVICE_ASSET_TAG0=9876543210
MEMORY_DEVICE_PART_NUMBER0=M378A1K43CB2-CTD    
MEMORY_DEVICE_RANK0=1
MEMORY_DEVICE_CONFIGURED_SPEED_MTS0=2400
MEMORY_DEVICE_MINIMUM_VOLTAGE0=1.2
MEMORY_DEVICE_MAXIMUM_VOLTAGE0=1.2
MEMORY_DEVICE_CONFIGURED_VOLTAGE0=1.2
MEMORY_DEVICE_TOTAL_WIDTH1=64
MEMORY_DEVICE_DATA_WIDTH1=64
MEMORY_DEVICE_SIZE1=8589934592
MEMORY_DEVICE_FORM_FACTOR1=DIMM
MEMORY_DEVICE_LOCATOR1=ChannelA-DIMM1
MEMORY_DEVICE_BANK_LOCATOR1=BANK 1
MEMORY_DEVICE_TYPE1=DDR4
MEMORY_DEVICE_TYPE_DETAIL1=Synchronous
MEMORY_DEVICE_SPEED_MTS1=2400
MEMORY_DEVICE_MANUFACTURER1=859B
MEMORY_DEVICE_SERIAL_NUMBER1=A02550A6
MEMORY_DEVICE_ASSET_TAG1=9876543210
MEMORY_DEVICE_PART_NUMBER1=BLT8G4D26AFTA.16FBD 
MEMORY_DEVICE_RANK1=2
MEMORY_DEVICE_CONFIGURED_SPEED_MTS1=2400
MEMORY_DEVICE_MINIMUM_VOLTAGE1=1.2
MEMORY_DEVICE_MAXIMUM_VOLTAGE1=1.2
MEMORY_DEVICE_CONFIGURED_VOLTAGE1=1.2
MEMORY_DEVICE_PRESENT2=0
MEMORY_DEVICE_LOCATOR2=ChannelB-DIMM0
MEMORY_DEVICE_BANK_LOCATOR2=BANK 2
MEMORY_DEVICE_TYPE2=Unknown
MEMORY_DEVICE_TYPE_DETAIL2=None
MEMORY_DEVICE_MANUFACTURER2=Not Specified
MEMORY_DEVICE_SERIAL_NUMBER2=Not Specified
MEMORY_DEVICE_ASSET_TAG2=Not Specified
MEMORY_DEVICE_PART_NUMBER2=Not Specified
MEMORY_DEVICE_TOTAL_WIDTH3=64
MEMORY_DEVICE_DATA_WIDTH3=64
MEMORY_DEVICE_SIZE3=8589934592
MEMORY_DEVICE_FORM_FACTOR3=DIMM
MEMORY_DEVICE_LOCATOR3=ChannelB-DIMM1
MEMORY_DEVICE_BANK_LOCATOR3=BANK 3
MEMORY_DEVICE_TYPE3=DDR4
MEMORY_DEVICE_TYPE_DETAIL3=Synchronous
MEMORY_DEVICE_SPEED_MTS3=2400
MEMORY_DEVICE_MANUFACTURER3=859B
MEMORY_DEVICE_SERIAL_NUMBER3=A0254F38
MEMORY_DEVICE_ASSET_TAG3=9876543210
MEMORY_DEVICE_PART_NUMBER3=BLT8G4D26AFTA.16FBD 
MEMORY_DEVICE_RANK3=2
MEMORY_DEVICE_CONFIGURED_SPEED_MTS3=2400
MEMORY_DEVICE_MINIMUM_VOLTAGE3=1.2
MEMORY_DEVICE_MAXIMUM_VOLTAGE3=1.2
MEMORY_DEVICE_CONFIGURED_VOLTAGE3=1.2
```

It supports using dmidecode generated dumps, for testing purposes.

The overall patch needs the udev rule actually tested, and probably more coding style fixes. I also don't find the property names particularly readable for the RAM modules, ideas welcome.
